examples: These animals were not as sensitive to climate changes in the Cretaceous period as they are today.
Scientists have linked the mass extinction at the end of the Cretaceous with a meteorite impact on Earth.
This change in plankton found in marine deposits is what marks the boundary between the Cretaceous and the Tertiary.
The fossils ranged from small Eocene swimmers imprinted on rock to the remains of late-Cretaceous dinosaurs.
One confounding factor is that the late Cretaceous was also a period of great volcanic activity.
